title: Adaptive Management Cycle
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive simulation of the six-step adaptive management cycle with three ecological scenarios and decision-based branching

learningObjectives: Implement the adaptive management cycle for an ecological management scenario
Identify the six steps of adaptive management and explain the purpose of each
Analyze how decisions at one step cascade through subsequent steps
Evaluate trade-offs between ecological effectiveness, cost, and social acceptance

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Scenario-based decision making with iterative learning cycles
keyQuestions: Why does ecological management require multiple cycles rather than a single plan?
How do monitoring data inform adjustments in the next cycle?
What trade-offs exist between aggressive intervention and cautious approaches?
How does uncertainty in ecosystems make adaptive management necessary?
commonMisconceptions: Ecological management is a one-time fix (it requires ongoing cycles of learning)
More aggressive interventions always produce better results (they often have unintended consequences)
Monitoring is optional or just a formality (it is the key learning step that drives adaptation)
assessmentOpportunities: Compare outcomes from different decision paths through the same scenario
Ask students to design their own adaptive management plan for a local ecological issue
Have students explain how Cycle 2 should differ from Cycle 1 based on monitoring data
